problems.title: Creating Dictionary from Lists
problems.description: Creating Dictionary from Lists. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.starter_code:
def main(keys, values):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['Navin', 'Kiran', 'Harish']

    values = ['Python', 'Java', 'JS']
    main(keys, values)
problems.solution:
def main(keys, values):
    my_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    print(my_dict)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['Navin', 'Kiran', 'Harish']

    values = ['Python', 'Java', 'JS']
    main(keys, values)
notes: Use zip() to create a dictionary from two lists.
Automatic test generation was skipped: unhashable type: 'list'

problems.title: Removing a Key-Value Pair
problems.description: Removing a Key-Value Pair. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.starter_code:
def main(keys, values, my_dict):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['Navin', 'Kiran', 'Harish']

    values = ['Python', 'Java', 'JS']

    my_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    main(keys, values, my_dict)
problems.solution:
def main(keys, values, my_dict):
    removed_value = my_dict.pop("Navin")
    print(removed_value)
    print(my_dict)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['Navin', 'Kiran', 'Harish']

    values = ['Python', 'Java', 'JS']

    my_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    main(keys, values, my_dict)
notes: Remove a key-value pair and capture the removed value.

problems.title: Dictionary Update
problems.description: Dictionary Update. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.starter_code:
def main(keys, values, my_dict):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['Navin', 'Kiran', 'Harish']

    values = ['Python', 'Java', 'JS']

    my_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    main(keys, values, my_dict)
problems.solution:
def main(keys, values, my_dict):
    my_dict.update({"Harish": "C++"})
    print(my_dict)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['Navin', 'Kiran', 'Harish']

    values = ['Python', 'Java', 'JS']

    my_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    main(keys, values, my_dict)
notes: Update dictionary with another dictionary.

problems.title: Creating Dictionary from Two Lists with zip
problems.description: Creating Dictionary from Two Lists with zip. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Creating Dictionary from Two Lists with zip
problems.starter_code:
def main(keys, values):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['one', 'two', 'three']

    values = [1, 2, 3]
    main(keys, values)
problems.solution:
def main(keys, values):
    combined_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    print(combined_dict)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['one', 'two', 'three']

    values = [1, 2, 3]
    main(keys, values)
notes: Create a dictionary by combining two lists using the zip function.
Automatic test generation was skipped: unhashable type: 'list'

problems.title: Extracting Lists from a Dictionary
problems.description: Extracting Lists from a Dictionary. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.starter_code:
def main(keys, values, combined_dict):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['one', 'two', 'three']

    values = [1, 2, 3]

    combined_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    main(keys, values, combined_dict)
problems.solution:
def main(keys, values, combined_dict):
    keys_list, values_list = zip(*combined_dict.items())
    print(list(keys_list))
    print(list(values_list))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    keys = ['one', 'two', 'three']

    values = [1, 2, 3]

    combined_dict = dict(zip(keys, values))
    main(keys, values, combined_dict)
notes: Extract separate lists of keys and values from a dictionary.
Automatic test generation was skipped: unhashable type: 'list'
